headline: Booking Stock, Expedia, DoorDash Jump. Here's What's Behind A 'Relief Rally.'
summary: Booking Holdings and other e-commerce stocks climbed Thursday on a report that OpenAI is "scaling back" plans to drive more shopping directly within ChatGPT.  Mizuho analyst Lloyd Walmsley cast the report as positive for several e-commerce stocks in a note to clients Thursday.  "We believe ChatGPT's pivot away from on-platform shopping checkout, if true, could spell the beginning of the end of major disruption fears for internet marketplace businesses, including OTAs (online travel agencies), DoorDash, etc.," Walmsley wrote.

headline: Software Stocks Are Roaring Back
summary: The Nasdaq Composite is riding a rally in software stocks back into positive territory. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF, or IGV, rallied 2.6% after joining in on Wednesday's risk-on rally.

headline: Mile Marker Celebrates One Year Launch Anniversary Capped by Impressive Growth, and the Unveiling of New NYC HQ
summary: Mile Marker, the independent media agency, is proud to celebrate its one-year anniversary, marking a year of 40% growth in 2025. Born from the merger of PlusMedia and Cage Point, Mile Marker hit the ground running with $500M in managed media and marquee brands, including Freshpet, DoorDash, and Webster Bank

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider DoorDash (DASH) After Its Recent Share Price Pullback
summary: Wondering if DoorDash at around US$177 a share still offers value or if most of the upside has already been priced in? This article breaks down what the current price could mean for you as an investor. The stock has been choppy, with a 2.7% move over the last week, a 14.4% decline over the last month, a 19.1% decline year to date, an 8.9% decline over 1 year, and a 205.4% gain over 3 years that leaves longer term holders in a very different position from recent buyers. headline: How The DoorDash (DASH) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Spending Plans And Analyst Revisions
summary: DoorDash’s fair value estimate has been trimmed from $274.69 to $260.90, a cut of about 5% in the latest model update. This shift lines up with Street research, where many firms are lowering price targets after recent Q4 results, Q1 guidance, and management’s plan for heavier investment through 2026, even as some keep positive ratings and others lean more cautious. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets fit into the evolving analyst story and how to keep track of it as an...
